verdicts.mechanism: `if not (major == 1)` applies `minor >= 21` to 2.x while skipping it for 1.x, causing false incompatibility warnings for urllib3 2.x and accepting pre-1.21 releases.
Outside TYPE_CHECKING, line 52 unconditionally returns True, so a PreparedRequest with request.url or request.method set to None is classified as _ValidatedRequest.

When conn.urlopen raises a MaxRetryError whose reason matches none of the raising branches (including NewConnectionError), line 729 passes and line 748 reads the never-assigned local resp, causing UnboundLocalError.

In `except (_SSLError, _HTTPError)`, line 738 raises `SSLError` only when `e` is not `_SSLError`, so ordinary `_HTTPError` subclasses are mislabeled while actual urllib3 `_SSLError` reaches the bare `raise`.
Because TYPE_CHECKING is True during static analysis, `if not TYPE_CHECKING` skips importing `Unpack` and `_t`, leaving every annotation that uses them unresolved to type checkers.
For any 4xx response lacking WWW-Authenticate, r.headers.get(...) at line 291 returns None and line 293 unconditionally calls s_auth.lower(), raising AttributeError.
For qop="auth-int, auth", qop.split(",") yields ["auth-int", " auth"], so line 243 misses the valid auth token and line 248 aborts.
At runtime TYPE_CHECKING is False, so line 48 enters `import chardet` and never calls the fallback resolver, raising ImportError when only charset_normalizer is installed.
MockRequest.get_header accepts default but calls self._r.headers.get(name) without passing it, so an absent header returns None even when a non-None default was supplied.
MockResponse.getheaders calls self._headers.getheaders(name) without returning its value, so every invocation implicitly returns None.
The condition `cookie.domain in domains` returns True only upon a repeated non-None domain, while distinct domains are merely appended and ultimately return False.
Line 516 evaluates set(kwargs) + set(result), but sets do not support +, so every create_cookie call raises TypeError before validating or creating the cookie.
The negated guard makes line 34 execute when response is None (raising AttributeError) and when self.request is explicitly truthy (overwriting it with response.request).
When the import fails OpenSSL is set to None, but `if not (OpenSSL)` enters the block and evaluates `OpenSSL.__version__`, raising AttributeError; successful imports skip metadata.

When `pending` is set for an incomplete line, line 1017 evaluates `(pending) - (chunk)`, and subtraction is unsupported for both `bytes` and `str`, so the next chunk raises `TypeError` instead of joining them.
Line 1074 tests `self.encoding is not None`, causing line 1075 to overwrite an explicit encoding, while a missing encoding bypasses detection and falls back to UTF-8 at line 1079.
Lines 1116–1117 catch and discard JSONDecodeError from the detected-encoding parse, then lines 1119–1120 retry via self.text instead of converting and raising RequestsJSONDecodeError immediately.

The `if not (mod == target or mod.startswith(f"{target}."))` branch aliases every non-target `sys.modules` entry and excludes `target` and its submodules, exactly inverting the intended chardet traversal.

Line 324 negates the entire presence-and-strip predicate, so line 327 retains Authorization exactly when should_strip_auth is true and otherwise attempts deletion even when the header is absent, causing KeyError.
Line 787 computes preferred_clock() + start, so two absolute timestamps are summed instead of calculating preferred_clock() - start, yielding an incorrect r.elapsed.
Line 122 applies the unsupported binary '-' operator to two str objects whenever __doc__ is non-None, so _init() raises TypeError during import.
Lines 55–57 set `data = {}` precisely when `data is not None`, discarding supplied entries, while `None` reaches `self.update(data, **kwargs)` and causes `MutableMapping.update` to fail.
LookupDict.get accepts default but line 130 calls self.__dict__.get(key) without passing it, so an absent key returns None instead of the caller-provided default.

The `except BaseException` surrounding both `yield tmp_handler` and `os.replace(...)` deletes the temporary file and then `pass`es, so write or replacement failures are normally swallowed and the context exits successfully.
Line 564 slices from idx - 1, so `charset=utf-8` yields `t=utf-8`, which line 584 returns as an invalid encoding name.
